observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer to coordinate their efforts in securing the Bank Statements on time that will
result in the timely submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements within the
prescribed period of ten (10) days after the end of the month as required under COA
Circular No. 96-011.

We recommend that Management direct the Municipal engineer to strictly
comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 as regards notification or publicity of the
projects of the Municipality.

We recommend that Management submit the purchase orders and other contracts within the five- day reglementary period from the execution and issuance thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders (POs)/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement of goods within five (5) working days from the execution thereof and within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively, pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

1.8 We recommend that Management submit the purchase orders and other
contracts within the five-day reglementary period from the execution and
issuance thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management comply with the submission of a Solid Waste Management Plan showing the required components for a ten (10)- year plan pursuant to Republic Act (R.A) No. 9003 otherwise known as Philippine Solid Waste Management Act.

We recommended that Management submit Purchase Orders and contracts within the five (5)-day period after execution and issuance thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

1.6 We recommended that Management comply with the submission of a Solid
Waste Management Plan showing the required components for a ten (10)-
year plan pursuant to Republic Act (R.A) No. 9003 otherwise known as
Philippine Solid Waste Management Act.

2.5 We recommended that Management comply with the submission to the
Auditor’s Office copies of Purchase Orders(POs)/Contracts and Acceptance
and Inspection Reports (AIRs) relative to the procurement of goods within
five (5) working days from the execution thereof and within twenty-four
(24) hours from acceptance of goods, respectively, pursuant to COA Circular
Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 in order that the Confirmation Letter, duly signed and approved by the Regional Director, be the one submitted to the Auditor to acquire the status of a bonded accountable officer.

We recommended that Management comply with sub-paragraph c (24) Section 12 of Republic Act No. 10121, or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010 regarding submission of report on the utilization of the DRRM fund.

We recommended that Management formulate a concrete Waste Management Plan in accordance with the “Ecological Waste Management Act of 2000” or R.A. 9003 by setting guidelines and targets for solid waste avoidance and volume reduction through waste reduction at source and waste minimization measures, and embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 in order that the Confirmation Letter, duly signed and approved by the Regional Director, be the one submitted to the Auditor to acquire the status of a bonded accountable officer.

We recommended that Management comply with sub-paragraph c (24) Section 12 of Republic Act No. 10121 or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010 regarding submission of report on the utilization of the DRRM fund.

We recommended that Management submit Purchase Orders and other contracts within the five (5) -day period after execution and issuance thereof as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that Management comply strictly with the guidelines of R.A. No. 10121 or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010 regarding transfer of unutilized balance of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to Trust Fund.

The management should follow strictly the guidelines set under R.A. No. 10121, the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010, on the transfer to the Trust Fund of the unutilized balance of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.

The Management should formulate a concrete waste management plan in accordance with the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” or R.A. 9003 by setting guidelines and targets for solid waste avoidance and volume reduction through waste reduction at source and waste minimization measures, and embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment.

We recommend that the concerned officers should strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the requirement to submit the list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year containing the following information:

We recommended that Management comply strictly with the guidelines of R.A. No. 10121 or the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010 regarding transfer of unutilized balance of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management formulate a concrete Waste Management Plan in accordance with the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” or R.A. 9003 by setting guidelines and targets for solid waste avoidance and volume reduction through waste reduction at source and waste minimization measures, and embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment.

The management should create an Inventory Committee to be responsible in the conduct of physical inventory-taking of the assets owned by the municipality as well as the preparation of the required reports pertaining thereto and to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

Submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the Bank Statements including debit/credit memos (DM/CM), paid checks, etc., to the Auditor should be made within five (5) days after the end of each month.

Submission of contracts and Purchase Orders within the five-day period after execution and issuance thereof, respectively, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 should be strictly adhered to by the management.

The management should create an Inventory Committee to be responsible in the conduct of physical inventory-taking of the assets owned by the municipality as well as the preparation of the required reports pertaining thereto and to submit the same to the Office of the Auditor.

Submission of the Bank Reconciliation Statements together with the Bank Statements including debit/credit memos (DM/CM), paid checks, etc., to the Auditor should be made within five (5) days after the end of each month

The management should follow strictly the guidelines set under R.A. No. 10121, the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010, on the transfer to the Trust Fund of the unutilized balance of the Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund.

The Management should formulate a concrete waste management plan in accordance with the “Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000” or R.A. 9003 by setting guidelines and targets for solid waste avoidance and volume reduction through waste reduction at source and waste minimization measures, and embark on effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s ecological solid waste management program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment.

Submission of contracts and Purchase Orders within the five-day period after execution and issuance thereof, respectively, as required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 should be strictly adhered to by the management.
